# Kashif Nazir

> Senior Technical Architect writing about cloud architecture, application compatibility, platform modernisation, and learning in public. Based in the UK.

Kash is a Senior Technical Architect at Cloudhouse, specialising in application compatibility, migration planning, and platform engineering. The site documents his learning journey across AWS, Kubernetes, infrastructure as code, Python, and Linux — with a focus on architecture decisions and the reasoning behind them.

## Blog Posts

- [Why I Couldn't Containerise My Home Server](https://kashifnazir.com/blog/why-i-couldnt-containerise-my-home-server/): A 2013-era i5 meets Docker, WSL, and the reality that hardware ceilings only show up when you try something new.
- [Watching AI Learn to Play Red Light, Green Light](https://kashifnazir.com/blog/using-ai-reinforcement-learning/): Trying AI Warehouse's Red Light, Green Light simulator and watching reinforcement learning agents shift from chaos to strategy.
- [Pilot to Production: The Gap Nobody Plans For](https://kashifnazir.com/blog/pilot-to-production-gap-nobody-plans-for/): Why the difficult part of migrations and AI rollouts is usually the jump from a supported pilot to organisation-wide adoption.
- [The Architecture Unbuilt](https://kashifnazir.com/blog/architecture-unbuilt/): What was considered for hosting, why GitHub Pages won for now, the planned AWS migration stack, and thoughts on designing for AI agents and llms.txt.
- [Building This Site](https://kashifnazir.com/blog/building-this-site/): How the site was built in two weeks using Hugo, two AI agents (Claude and Codex), and zero web development experience.
- [AI for AWS Certs](https://kashifnazir.com/blog/ai-for-aws-certs/): Using custom GPTs to study for AWS certifications — prompt design, system instructions, and what actually works.

## Projects

- [Personal Site Build Log](https://kashifnazir.com/projects/site-build-log/): Tracking the first build of the personal site and the decisions behind it.

## About

- [About](https://kashifnazir.com/about/): Background, experience, and what this site is for.
